name: encrypted-secrets
scenario: Store sensitive credentials (database password, API secret key, JWT secret) as encrypted secrets. Retrieve them and verify decryption works. Demonstrate the machine-binding limitation by explaining why secrets can't be migrated between machines. Show how v1/v2 secrets auto-migrate to v3.
success_criteria: Uses set --secret flag for storing encrypted values
Uses --json for verification (with stderr warning handling)
Does not log secret values to history
anti_patterns: Not handling stderr warnings when using --json
Attempting to migrate secrets between machines without re-encryption
security_notes: Secrets are encrypted with HKDF + HMAC-CTR (v3 format)
Encryption keys derived from machine identity (hostname + uid + arch)
Secrets cannot be migrated between machines
History does not log secret values
